title: Page & PageWithSidebar
intro: `wf-page` is the outermost shell every wireframe starts from — a full-height flex column that stacks a `wf-page-header` (title block + actions) over a `wf-page-content` region. Add `wf-page-with-sidebar` and the column becomes a row: a persistent `wf-sidebar` beside a `wf-main` that holds the same header/content pair. These are the two scaffolds DESIGN.md 5.5 funnels every page pattern through.

blocks.body:
Every wireframe page begins with `wf-page`: a `display: flex; flex-direction: column` container at `min-height: 100vh` that paints `--wf-color-background-subtle` and owns the page font and primary text color. Inside it, the three regions are fixed in role — a `wf-page-header` bar on top, a `wf-page-content` region that takes `flex: 1` and grows to fill the rest.

The header is a `space-between` row: a `wf-page-title` column on the left (h1 + optional `wf-text-helper` subtitle) and a `wf-page-actions` row on the right that pins its buttons with `flex-shrink: 0` so they never wrap under pressure. The header sits on its own `--wf-color-background` surface with a `--wf-color-border` bottom rule, so it reads as chrome above the content.

When the page needs top-level navigation, add `wf-page-with-sidebar` to the same `wf-page` element. That single modifier flips the flex direction to `row`, and you nest a `wf-sidebar` (brand header, scrollable nav, user footer) ahead of a `wf-main` wrapper that carries the header + content pair. `wf-main` is `flex: 1` with `min-width: 0` so a wide grid inside it can't blow out the layout.

blocks.title: Basic Page Structure
blocks.caption: The canonical scaffold: a wf-page-header (wf-page-title with h1 + subtitle on the left, wf-page-actions on the right) above a wf-page-content region. The header sits on its own white surface with a bottom border; content grows to fill.

blocks.title: Page skeleton
blocks.code:
Dashboard

Welcome back! Here's what's happening today.
blocks.note: The title block is a column (h1 stacked on the helper subtitle); the actions block is a non-shrinking row. Omit wf-page-actions entirely for a title-only header.

blocks.title: Page regions
blocks.intro: The page scaffold is a fixed set of regions, not a prop API. Compose them in order — header, then content — and reach for the content padding modifiers when density calls for it.

rows.name: wf-page
rows.when: The outermost shell. Full-height flex column on --wf-color-background-subtle. One per page.

rows.name: wf-page-header
rows.when: The title-and-actions bar. space-between row on its own --wf-color-background surface with a bottom border.

rows.name: wf-page-title
rows.when: The left side of the header: a column holding the h1 and an optional wf-text-helper subtitle (gap --wf-space-1).

rows.name: wf-page-actions
rows.when: The right side of the header: an aligned row of buttons that flex-shrinks to zero so it never wraps.

rows.name: wf-page-content
rows.when: The main content region. flex: 1, --wf-space-6 (24px) padding. The default for almost every page.

rows.name: wf-page-content-tight
rows.when: Reduce content padding to --wf-space-4 (16px) for denser, grid-heavy pages.

rows.name: wf-page-content-spacious
rows.when: Loosen content padding to --wf-space-8 (32px) for an onboarding-shaped or marketing surface.

rows.name: wf-page-content-none
rows.when: Drop content padding to 0 when a full-bleed grid or table owns its own edges.

blocks.title: Full Example: Dashboard Page
blocks.caption: The scaffold composed into a real page: a header with date-range and download actions over a wf-page-content holding a wf-row of stat cards and a Recent Orders table inside a wf-card.

blocks.title: Page with Sidebar Shell
blocks.caption: The two-column app shell: a 256px wf-sidebar (brand header, wf-sidebar-nav, user footer) beside a wf-main that carries the page header and wf-page-content. wf-page-with-sidebar on the wf-page element flips the flex direction to row.

blocks.title: Page with sidebar skeleton
blocks.note: wf-page-with-sidebar lives on the same element as wf-page. The header/content pair moves inside wf-main — it does not sit directly under wf-page anymore.

blocks.title: Sidebar shell parts
blocks.intro: The sidebar is a three-zone column: a fixed-height brand header on top, a scrollable nav in the middle, and a footer pinned to the bottom. Beside it, wf-main wraps the page header and content.

rows.name: wf-page-with-sidebar
rows.when: Modifier on wf-page that flips flex-direction to row, turning the page into a two-column shell.

rows.name: wf-sidebar
rows.when: Fixed 256px-wide, full-height navigation column on --wf-color-background with a right border. flex-shrink: 0 so it holds its width.

rows.name: wf-sidebar-collapsed
rows.when: Narrow the sidebar to 64px for an icon-only rail. Pair with wf-sidebar.

rows.name: wf-sidebar-header
rows.when: Brand/logo zone at the top: a fixed 64px-tall row (centered, --wf-space-4 horizontal padding) with a bottom border.

rows.name: wf-sidebar-nav
rows.when: The scrollable middle. flex: 1 with overflow-y: auto and --wf-space-4 padding — nav items and sections live here.

rows.name: wf-sidebar-footer
rows.when: Pinned bottom zone with a top border, for the signed-in user or settings shortcut.

rows.name: wf-main
rows.when: The right column wrapper. flex: 1, column layout, min-width: 0 to prevent a wide grid from overflowing the shell. Holds the header + content.

blocks.title: Structural tokens
blocks.intro: The scaffold's fixed dimensions and surfaces. Padding and gaps come from the spacing scale; never write the literal alongside the token.

rows.use: wf-page background — the muted base the chrome sits on.
wf-page-header and wf-sidebar background — the chrome reads lighter than the page.
Header bottom rule, sidebar right border, sidebar header/footer dividers (with --wf-border-width).
Default page text color, set on wf-page.
wf-page-header padding and the default wf-page-content padding.
Header gap, sidebar nav/footer padding, and wf-page-content-tight.
Gap between the h1 and subtitle inside wf-page-title.
Fixed sidebar width; collapses to 64px with wf-sidebar-collapsed.
Fixed brand-zone height — the scaffold's only set-height region.

blocks.title: Scaffolding rules
blocks.intro: From DESIGN.md 5.5 and the layout source. Start from a scaffold, don't reinvent one.

items.rule: Start every page from one of the six pattern scaffolds in 5.5 (DashboardLayout, MasterDetailLayout, FormWizard, SettingsPage, DataTablePage, ComparisonMatrix), each built on wf-page.
items.why: If the page doesn't fit one, that's a signal to check whether it's a genuinely new pattern or a variant of an existing one — most of the time it's the latter.

items.rule: wf-page-with-sidebar is a modifier on wf-page, not a replacement for it.
items.why: It only flips flex-direction to row; the page still needs the wf-page base for height, background, font, and text color.

items.rule: When you add a sidebar, the header and content move inside wf-main — they no longer sit directly under wf-page.
items.why: wf-main is the flexing right column (min-width: 0) that absorbs a wide grid; putting the header under wf-page instead would stretch it across the sidebar.

items.rule: Keep wf-page-actions a non-wrapping row of buttons only — no filters or search.
items.why: It carries flex-shrink: 0 so primary actions stay reachable; filtering belongs in a FilterBar inside the content, not the header (4.3).

items.rule: Reach for the wf-page-content padding modifiers instead of inline padding.
items.why: Default 24px, tight 16px, spacious 32px, none 0 — all on the 8px grid (5.2). A custom value breaks the rhythm.

items.rule: One wf-page per page and one wf-page-header inside it.
items.why: The scaffold defines a single primary location and title; a second header reads as a second page.
wf-page-with-sidebar only sets flex-direction: row. Without wf-page there's no min-height, background, font, or text color — the shell collapses.

items.dont: Header + content directly under wf-page when a sidebar is present
items.why: wf-main is the flex: 1 / min-width: 0 column that sits beside the sidebar. Skip it and the header stretches across the whole row instead of just the content side.
The modifier already maps to --wf-space-4 (16px) on the grid. The token is the source of truth (2.7); the literal isn't.

items.dont: Subtitle as a second <h1> or a sized <span>
items.why: wf-page-title is a column with a --wf-space-1 gap built for an h1 + helper pair; a second heading breaks the type hierarchy.

items.title: The sidebar goes fixed-and-hidden on mobile
items.detail: At max-width 640px the layout.css moves wf-sidebar to position: fixed; left: -100% and reveals it with the wf-sidebar-open class (left: 0). Below that breakpoint you need a toggle to add wf-sidebar-open, or the nav is off-screen.

items.title: wf-page-header restacks under 640px
items.detail: The same media query flips wf-page-header to flex-direction: column; align-items: stretch and right-aligns wf-page-actions. The title-over-actions stack is automatic — don't fight it with inline flex overrides.

items.title: Component demos lean on demo-only classes
items.detail: Page.html and PageWithSidebar.html render buttons, placeholders, and nav items with page-local helpers (demo-btn, demo-placeholder, demo-nav-item) defined in their inline <style>. Those are scaffolding props for the demo, not part of layout.css — use the real wf- button/nav components in a build.

items.title: wf-main carries min-width: 0 on purpose
items.detail: Without it, a wide table or grid inside wf-page-content forces the flex item past the viewport and the sidebar gets squeezed. Keep min-width: 0 on wf-main when you swap in your own markup.

items.title: wf-sidebar-header height is locked to 64px
items.detail: It's a fixed 64px-tall row (the only fixed-height region in the scaffold; the page header is padding-driven, not a set height). A taller brand lockup will clip or push the divider — scale the logo down rather than growing the header.
